rationale: z3 stalls because the two ensures-conjuncts unfold `SingleDelivery::send_single_message` along disjoint `(post, sm)` chains and never align the `if new_seqno > params.max_seqno` case-split shared between the r1- and r2-runs. I bind the shared `(pre_v, post1_v, post2_v, params, old_ack, new_seqno, cond)` once, then state the alignment as quantified implications over `(post, sm)`: (A) instantiates the deterministic open-spec body on both sides so any `Some` witness forces `!cond` and pins `sm`/`post` to the else-branch expression, while any `None` witness forces `cond` and pins `post == pre`; (B)/(C) pin matching tags to identical pre_v-derived expressions; (D) rules out mismatched tags as contradictory; (E) lifts `SingleDelivery` view-equality (extensional via `#[verifier::ext_equal]`) to per-component `receive_state@`/`send_state@`; (F) pushes the equality through the `map_values` wrappers used by `CTombstoneTable::view`/`CSendState::view` to the underlying `epmap@` Map views, exposing the extensional-equality bridge that `det_send_single_cmessage_equal` projects onto.
